new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine produced in the French province of Champagne from grapes exclusively harvested in that province, classified under CN code 2204 10 11 (Chapter 22).

Quality representing that a sparkling wine originates from the French province of Champagne and is produced exclusively from grapes harvested in that province, as required for classification under CN code 2204 10 11.

coverage_explanation: The two key distinguishing criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) Champagne is a sparkling wine (modelled via subClassOf eucn:SparklingWine), and (2) it originates exclusively from grapes harvested in the French province of Champagne (modelled via the ChampagneRegionOrigin quality and the hasGeographicOriginQuality restriction). The CN code 2204 10 11 is anchored via the cnHeadingCode hasValue restriction. A minor gap (score reduced to 0.9) is that the secondary fermentation method specific to Champagne (méthode champenoise / in-bottle secondary fermentation) is not explicitly modelled, as this technical production detail is not present in the provided legal text and the existing TBox does not contain a suitable production-method property at this level of granularity.
